stories_covered: SpaceX completes largest IPO in history, hits $2.7 trillion valuation
Anthropic launches Claude Fable Monday, US government bans it by Thursday on export restriction grounds
Salesforce acquires Finn (formerly Intercom) for $3.6 billion
Adobe beats and raises guidance but stock falls 6% on CFO exit
Wix slashes 2026 guidance, cuts 1,000 employees and revenue outlook by $25 million
harry_takes: Elon perfectly timed SpaceX IPO at 19% day-one pop despite zero price discovery—designed pop execution
This Fable ban is a Rubicon moment: first US capability-based AI model regulation, potentially opening door to gating superintelligence by nation
Both Anthropic (claiming danger) and the administration (claiming sovereignty) are politically trapped in contradictory positions with no good communication
Tim Cook's operational excellence extended to his own exit—managed succession with barely-moved stock price
Owen McDonough and Intercom proved the golden path: take pre-AI SaaS, burn the boats, triple growth—Salesforce recognized it as blue ocean
harry_boldest_quote: It was an amazing outcome and you said it's traded well, it's traded way more than well.
predictions: Evan Stude: SpaceX stock will trade below current price in six months due to gamma squeeze and low float volatility, despite company fundamentals
Panel: If government treats Fable ban narrowly (Anthropic only), not broadly (all models), Anthropic has due-process claim
Rory: Anthropic IPO happens in Q4 2026 despite Fable ban if political clarity comes within 6-12 months
Evan: Foreign open-source models (Chinese) will dominate as US restricts frontier models—sovereignty play creates 2-3 player oligopoly
disagreements: Harry believed Fable ban was primarily political miscommunication; Evan emphasized real technical capability risk. Both agreed it doesn't matter much to Anthropic IPO fundamentals if resolved quickly.
harry_role: Asking precise questions on SpaceX gamma squeeze mechanics and Fable ban implications while Evan (a top early-stage investor) provides institutional counterweight and sovereign AI market analysis.
notable: Evan Stude frames Fable ban as Rubicon moment for capability-based AI regulation and sovereign AI—first time US has gated a model on what it can do, not what buyer can do with it, creating geopolitical precedent.
